reviewed_scope: Full project diff from base ref 6dd693f91eb10f37919189838085b632baefbb13 to HEAD, including committed Forge steps 1-10 and final blocker fixes. Mandatory Forge context and prior reviews were read. Reviewed changed-file inventory, map API/MQTT store, Docker/Compose runtime files, GHCR workflow, branding shell, tools, sitemap/docs, and final grep guards.

unresolved_step_flags.note: Browser fetch generally cannot set a custom User-Agent header for Nominatim; non-blocking because lookup failure is handled.
The Summary serial action reads potentially sensitive values; acceptable for release but consider a future warning.
CSP remains static and may need expansion if future runtime tile or browser-side connection providers are introduced.

plan_alignment.notes: The redesign, rebrand, /api/map/* replacement, tools routes, hard-removal, Docker runtime, and GHCR workflow are largely aligned. However, root/contribution licensing still claims MIT despite GPL-derived map compliance work, and Compose defaults can mask configured MQTT with sample data, which conflicts with the Docker-primary runtime/MQTT requirements.

correctness_safety.notes: The previous MQTT adapter blocker is resolved in the API/store path, including Node dynamic runtime and connection-state reporting. Blocking issue remains because the primary Compose example forces sample data on by default, so a configured MQTT URL can be ignored unless users also know to set MESHCORE_MAP_SAMPLE_DATA=false.

code_quality.notes: The reviewed implementation is generally clean, typed, modular, and consistent with App Router and shared component patterns.

completeness.notes: Most step acceptance criteria and reported validations are complete. Release-compliance and Docker runtime documentation/configuration are not complete until the license conflict and sample-data default are fixed.

patterns.notes: The implementation follows the planned centralized constants, map data contracts, client/server separation, and Docker standalone patterns.

integration.notes: Imports/exports and route removals integrate cleanly, but Compose/.env defaults currently undermine the live MQTT runtime integration path.

release_readiness.notes: Do not release with conflicting MIT/GPL licensing metadata and a Docker-primary Compose example that can silently serve sample map data despite MQTT being configured.

flags.note: NEXT_PUBLIC_MAP_TILE_URL is documented and parsed in config, but the map uses a hard-coded CARTO tile URL. This is not release-blocking if CARTO is the intended provider, but the docs/env variable should be reconciled.
Package name remains denvermc. This is not visible site branding, but consider renaming package metadata in a follow-up.

required_changes.issue: Root repository license still declares MIT, while the released image and map attribution now state GPL-3.0-or-later for live-map-derived work. This creates conflicting release/legal metadata.
required_changes.fix: Update repository licensing to a GPL-compatible license for the combined work, or add clear multi-license/compliance notices that accurately identify GPL-covered portions. Also remove the stale Denver MeshCore copyright.
required_changes.plan_ref: PLAN.md Cross-Step Integration Checks requires GPL attribution/source compliance for live-map-derived work.

required_changes.issue: Contributing guidance says contributions are licensed under MIT, conflicting with the Docker image GPL license label and live-map GPL compliance note.
required_changes.fix: Update the contribution license statement to match the corrected repository licensing/compliance model.
required_changes.plan_ref: Step 9/final fixes corrected OCI licensing to GPL-3.0-or-later; docs must not contradict release metadata.

required_changes.issue: Compose forces MESHCORE_MAP_SAMPLE_DATA to true by default. Because getMapSnapshot checks sampleData before mqttConfigured, a user can configure MESHCORE_MQTT_URL in the primary Docker runtime and still receive sample data unless they also set MESHCORE_MAP_SAMPLE_DATA=false.
required_changes.fix: Do not force sample data to true in Compose/.env examples. Let the app default apply when the variable is unset or blank, and document explicit true only for demo mode. Also adjust /Users/cjvana/Documents/GitHub/denvermc-org/.env.example line 11 accordingly.
required_changes.plan_ref: Step 8 requires Compose runtime environment configuration; Step 10 final fixes require configured MQTT ingestion to be usable in the Docker-primary runtime.
